title: Commodities Brief — 15 May 2026
intro:
COMMODITIES BRIEF
15 May 2026 • 05:55 CET

Commodities Markets — Session Overview

Risk-off sentiment dominates early European trading as precious metals retreat sharply while energy and agriculture extend gains. The dollar's strength continues pressuring metals amid evolving Fed policy expectations, though the broader commodities supercycle thesis remains intact on structural supply constraints.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
COMMODITIES BRIEF
15 May 2026 • 05:55 CET

Commodities Markets — Session Overview

Risk-off sentiment dominates early European trading as precious metals retreat sharply while energy and agriculture extend gains. The dollar's strength continues pressuring metals amid evolving Fed policy expectations, though the broader commodities supercycle thesis remains intact on structural supply constraints.

Precious Metals
Gold retreats to $4572.80/oz (-2.25% intraday, -3.09% weekly) as real rates climb following stronger-than-expected US data. Central bank buying remains supportive at current levels. S

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
ilver underperforms at $78.48/oz (-7.57% intraday), reflecting industrial demand concerns. Platinum trades $2005.30/oz (-3.75%) while palladium weakens to $1417.50/oz amid automotive sector headwinds. Copper's decline to $6.35/lb (-3.25%) signals growth concerns as China's property sector remains subdued.

Energy
WTI crude advances to $103.17/bbl (+1.98%) on renewed Middle East tensions and OPEC+ production discipline. Brent strengthens to $107.44/bbl (+1.63%) with the spread widening to $4.27/bbl, reflecting regional supply disruptions. Natural gas holds steady at $2.92/MMBtu (+1.00%) on stor

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
age dynamics.

Agriculture
Wheat rallies to $656.75/bu (+1.51%) on Black Sea export concerns. Corn surges $469.75/bu (+4.04%) following adverse weather forecasts in key growing regions. Soybeans edge higher to $1194.50/bu (+1.70%) despite South American harvest pressures.

Trade Ideas
LONG: WTI crude (geopolitical premium), wheat (supply risks), corn (weather premium)
SHORT/HEDGE: Silver (industrial weakness), copper (growth slowdown)

Key Risks
Fed policy pivot impacting dollar strength; escalating Middle East tensions disrupting energy flows; adverse weather threatening crop yields globally.
